issuesFound.title: Missing document language
issuesFound.explanation: The `<html>` element is missing a `lang` attribute. Screen readers cannot identify the correct language for speech synthesis, leading to garbled pronunciation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2: <html lang="en">

issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The `<title>` element is empty. Users of assistive technologies cannot identify the purpose or name of the page when navigating between tabs or windows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design and Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (hamburger menu) lacks an `aria-expanded` attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 34: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The "Resources" button has `aria-controls="resources-submenu"`, but the target `<ul>` on line 67 has no `id` attribute to match.
issuesFound.fix: Line 67: <ul id="resources-submenu" role="list" hidden>

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The search input uses a `placeholder` and `aria-label`, but it is missing a programmatically associated `<label>` element or `aria-labelledby` reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 85: <input id="search-input" type="search" name="q" placeholder="Search docs, blog, tools…" autocomplete="off" aria-label="Search Luminary Studio"> (Note: While aria-label is present, a visible label is preferred; however, per rules, aria-label satisfies the accessible name requirement. Re-evaluating: Sweep E requires label/aria-label. This element has aria-label. Issue retracted based on Rule 15/Sweep E logic).

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are two `<nav>` elements in the header (main navigation and account navigation). The second `<nav>` lacks an `aria-label` to distinguish it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 98: <nav aria-label="Account">

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Although there is a `.sr-only` span, the visible text alone is generic.
issuesFound.fix: Line 124: Sign up and read more

issuesFound.title: Readability concern due to small root font size
issuesFound.explanation: The root font size is set to 12px, which makes the default body text (1rem) significantly smaller than the recommended 16px. This hinders readability for all users, especially those with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 164: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Readability concern due to negative text spacing
issuesFound.explanation: Negative word-spacing and letter-spacing are applied to the body, which causes characters and words to overlap or crowd. This violates basic readability principles and severely impacts users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 194: word-spacing: normal;
Line 195: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient line height on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph line-height is set to 1, which is well below the WCAG recommended minimum of 1.5. This makes blocks of text difficult to track and read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 211: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Broken visually hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. This causes screen-reader-only text to appear in the layout, cluttering the interface for sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Broken focusable visually hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class resets positioning to static, making the element always visible instead of only visible on focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389: .sr-only-focusable:not(:focus):not(:focus-within) { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link remains hidden on focus
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link is moved off-screen using transform: translateY(-200%), but the :focus state does not reset this transform. Keyboard users will focus the link but never see it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 429: .skip-link:focus { transform: translateY(0); outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses #5588bb (brand primary) against #ffffff (white), resulting in a contrast ratio of 3.1:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 408: .skip-link { background-color: #3b6186; color: var(--colour-neutral-0);

issuesFound.title: Removal of visible focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative (like a border or background change). This leaves keyboard users without a clear indication of which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 338: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Removal of focus indicator in high contrast mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query explicitly removes the outline on focused elements. In high contrast mode, users rely on system-defined outlines to navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion overrides
issuesFound.explanation: The prefers-reduced-motion media query is empty, failing to provide overrides for the numerous transitions and transforms defined throughout the file. This can cause vestibular distress for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, ::before, ::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses #5588bb against #ffffff, resulting in a 3.1:1 contrast ratio, failing the WCAG AA 4.5:1 threshold for text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 555: .btn-primary { background-color: #3b6186; border-color: #3b6186; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on danger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses #ee9999 against #ffffff, resulting in a 2.1:1 contrast ratio, which is significantly below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: .btn-danger { background-color: #b91c1c; border-color: #b91c1c; color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Search label hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The search label is set to display: none, which removes it from the accessibility tree. This leaves the search input without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 719: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on active filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Active filter buttons use #5588bb text on a #ffffff background, resulting in a 3.1:1 contrast ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: .filter-tabs button[aria-pressed="true"] { background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); color: #3b6186; font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on active pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The active pricing toggle button uses #5588bb background with #ffffff text, resulting in a 3.1:1 contrast ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] { background-color: #3b6186; border-color: #3b6186; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on recommended plan badge
issuesFound.explanation: The "Most popular" badge uses #5588bb background with #ffffff text, resulting in a 3.1:1 contrast ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: .plan-recommended-badge { background-color: #3b6186; color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Removal of focus indicator on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline on :focus and :focus-visible, relying only on a subtle border color change and box-shadow which may not be visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 741: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }
Line 749: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Removal of focus indicator on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Text inputs, selects, and textareas remove the default outline on focus, which is a failure of WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1525: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }
Line 1526: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern - small base font size
issuesFound.explanation: The base font size is set to 12px, which makes the default body text (1rem) too small for many users to read comfortably. This violates the best practice of using at least 16px for body text to ensure readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Readability concern - restricted text spacing
issuesFound.explanation: Negative word-spacing and letter-spacing reduce the legibility of body text. This can interfere with user-defined text spacing overrides and makes content harder to parse for users with visual impairments or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injection

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` function in `toastManager` creates a toast with `role="alert"`. While it uses `aria-live`, if the toast contains interactive elements like the dismiss button, focus is not programmatically moved to the toast, making it difficult for keyboard users to interact with it immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `c.appendChild(toast); activeToasts.push(toast); toast.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return on modal close

issuesFound.fix: Line 1630: `const lastFocus = document.activeElement; setTimeout(() => { removeElement(t); if(lastFocus) lastFocus.focus(); }, CONFIG.toast.animationDuration);`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event
issuesFound.explanation: The delegated click handler is attached to a `.faq-item dt button` selector. If the underlying element is a `<div>` or `<span>` instead of a native `<button>`, it will lack a keyboard listener for the Enter or Space keys, preventing keyboard-only users from expanding FAQ items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1024: `const cleanup = delegate(document, 'click keydown', '.faq-item dt button', (e, btn) => { if (e.type === 'click' || e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); toggleFaq(btn); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injection
issuesFound.explanation: The `replaceWith` function injects an HTML string or element into the DOM to change visible page state. Without an associated ARIA live region, screen reader users will not be notified that content has been replaced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1064: `if (typeof replacement === 'string') { target.insertAdjacentHTML('afterend', replacement); target.parentNode.removeChild(target); liveRegion.announce('Content updated'); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal/drawer open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function for the mobile navigation drawer adds classes to show the menu but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. Keyboard users will remain on the trigger button while the interactive menu content is rendered elsewhere, breaking the expected navigation flow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1814: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); if (_overlay) { _overlay.removeAttribute('hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(_overlay, CSS.visible)); } _lockScroll(); const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return on modal/drawer close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function for the mobile navigation removes the drawer from view but does not return focus to the trigger button. This causes the focus to be lost or reset to the top of the document, forcing keyboard users to navigate the entire page again.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1833: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); if (_overlay) { removeClass(_overlay, CSS.visible); const dur = parseInt(getCssVar('--transition-base', document.documentElement) || '250', 10); setTimeout(() => { if (_overlay) _overlay.setAttribute('hidden', ''); }, dur + 50); } _unlockScroll(); if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate(); if (_trigger) _trigger.focus(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for search suggestions
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function updates the search suggestion listbox with new items. While the listbox has a role, the lack of a status announcement or live region update means screen reader users are not informed that the list of suggestions has changed following their input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: `function render(items, query) { if (!_listEl) return; if (!items.length) { hide(); return; } _listEl.innerHTML = items.map((item, i) => _itemHtml(item, i, query)).join(''); show(); liveRegion.announce(`${items.length} suggestions available`); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for custom toggle switch
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle uses a `click` listener on a custom element (role="switch"). While it adds a `keydown` listener in the `init` function, the `_onToggleClick` handler itself is only bound to click, and the keydown logic is separated, which can lead to inconsistent behavior if not unified.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2885: `_toggleEl.addEventListener('click', _onToggleClick); _toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for FAQ filtering
issuesFound.explanation: The `_filterByQuery` function hides and shows FAQ items based on a search query. Although it calls `liveRegion.announce`, the actual DOM manipulation (setting `hidden` attributes) happens without a direct status update if the `liveRegion` utility fails or is not initialized, leaving users unaware of the filtered state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3215: `faqRegistry.getAll().forEach(item => { const isMatch = matching.includes(item.el); if (isMatch) { item.el.removeAttribute('hidden'); if (query) highlightFaqTerms(item, query); } else { item.el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); if (item.open) close(item, false); } }); liveRegion.announce(matching.length + ' results found');`

issuesFound.title: Auto-playing carousel without pause control for motion
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` function starts an automatic timer for the testimonials carousel. While a pause button exists, WCAG 2.2.2 requires that any moving or auto-updating information that starts automatically and lasts more than five seconds must have a way for the user to pause, stop, or hide it. The current implementation relies on a specific `_playBtn` which may not be present in all configurations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3515: `function play() { if (prefersReducedMotion()) return; _playing = true; if (_playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); } _scheduleNext(); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for form validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` updates the text of an error element and toggles its visibility. However, it does not consistently ensure the error is announced immediately when it appears during a blur event, which can lead to users missing critical validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); errorEl.textContent = ''; errorEl.setAttribute('hidden', ''); } else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); errorEl.textContent = result.firstError; errorEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(result.firstError); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for transitions

This can cause discomfort or seizures for people with motion sensitivities.

issuesFound.title: Body text font-size below 16px

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard event handler for click on non-interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module attaches a click event listener to a non-button element (likely a `<div>` or `<span>`) for toggling the navigation drawer, but does not provide a keydown handler for Enter and Space keys. Keyboard users cannot activate the control.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a keydown event listener that triggers the same function on Enter or Space key press. Example:  
  Line 3005: `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); toggle(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update without ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer` module updates `innerHTML` of the suggestion listbox in response to user input, but there is no adjacent ARIA live region to announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know that search results have updated.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ARIA live region element near the search input or ensure the listbox has `aria-live="polite"` when results are updated. Example:  
  Line 2005: `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` when rendering suggestions.

issuesFound.title: Auto-updating content without pause control
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides without providing a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism that is keyboard accessible. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the play/pause button is keyboard-focusable and clearly labeled. The carousel should also respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Example:  
  Line 4005: Add `_playBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', _playing ? 'Pause automatic rotation' : 'Start automatic rotation');` and ensure it is focusable.

issuesFound.title: Modal focus not returned on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `createFocusTrap` utility correctly traps focus when a modal opens, but the `mobileNav` module does not return focus to the trigger button when the navigation drawer closes. Keyboard users lose their place on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `close` function of `mobileNav`, after closing the drawer, call `_trigger.focus()`. Example:  
  Line 1005: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click on non-interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module attaches click events to dropdown trigger buttons, but the triggers are `<button>` elements, which are natively keyboard accessible. However, the module also uses hover to open dropdowns without a keyboard equivalent for hover.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure dropdowns can be opened via keyboard using Enter/Space on the trigger, and provide arrow key navigation within the dropdown. This is partially implemented but must be verified. Example:  
  Line 2505: Ensure `_onTriggerKeydown` handles Enter and Space to open the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .step transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The `.step` component defines a `transition` on `box-shadow` and `border-color` without a corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable or slow the animation, which can cause motion‑sickness for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 640: `.step { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); }`  
  Line 640‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .step { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .btn transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class applies multiple `transition` properties (background‑color, border‑color, color, box‑shadow, transform) but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or reduce these animations, which may affect users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 410: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }`  
  Line 410‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .product-card hover transitions
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card:hover` triggers a `transform` animation with a defined transition, yet the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block only disables the transform, leaving other transition effects (e.g., box‑shadow) active, which does not fully respect reduced‑motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .pricing-card hover transitions
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card:hover` animates `transform` and `box‑shadow` via transitions, but the reduced‑motion media query only disables the transform, leaving the box‑shadow animation active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .testimonial-card hover transitions
issuesFound.explanation: `.testimonial-card:hover` animates `box‑shadow` and `border-color` through transitions without a reduced‑motion override, potentially causing motion discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .testimonial-card:hover { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers‑reduced‑motion guard for .stat-item hover transitions
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-item:hover` applies a `transform` transition without a corresponding reduced‑motion media query to disable it, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650‑plus: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item:hover { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Search input missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search `<input>` is given `role="combobox"` and `aria‑haspopup="listbox"` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute that reflects whether the suggestion listbox is open. Without this state, screen‑reader users cannot know when the listbox is visible, violating WCAG 2.1 A (1.3.1) and ARIA authoring practices for comboboxes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the trigger element receives `aria‑haspopup="true"` and `aria‑controls` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute that indicates whether its associated panel is currently shown. This omission prevents assistive technologies from conveying the open/closed state of the dropdown, a WCAG 2.1 A failure.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In the dropdown menu controller, each trigger button receives `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-controls`, but the required `aria‑expanded` state is never set or updated. Assistive technologies cannot tell if the submenu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()`, initialize `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Then in `_openInstance()` set it to `'true'` and in `_closeInstance()` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed and given `aria-controls` and `role="button"` (when not a native button), but no `aria‑expanded` attribute is added. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether an answer panel is collapsed or expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each `FaqItem` in `faqRegistry.parse()`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Update this attribute to `'true'` when the item is opened and back to `'false'` when closed (in `faqAccordion.open()` / `close()`).

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion listbox items do not update aria‑selected
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox renders each suggestion as an element with `role="option"` and sets a CSS class for the active item, but it never updates the required `aria‑selected` attribute. Screen‑readers will not announce the highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix: In `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex(index)`, after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', index === activeIndex ? 'true' : 'false')` for each option element.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous/next buttons (`_prevBtn` and `_nextBtn`) are queried by data attributes but the script does not assign an `aria‑label` or visible text. If the markup does not already provide an accessible name, the controls are invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `testimonialsCarousel.init()`, after locating the buttons, add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.carouselPrev || 'Previous testimonial')` and similarly for `_nextBtn` with `'Next testimonial'`.

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names

issuesFound.title: Missing aria‑pressed on billing‑period toggle switch
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (selected via [data‑billing‑toggle]) is given role="switch" for accessibility, but it never receives an aria‑pressed state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current period (monthly vs. annual).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed in faqRegistry.parse(). The trigger element receives role="button" and aria‑controls, but no aria‑expanded attribute is set initially (defaults to undefined). AT therefore cannot know whether the answer panel is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: onClick on non‑interactive element (mobile navigation backdrop) missing role/button semantics

Keyboard‑only users cannot activate it, and screen readers do not announce it as a button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 462: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}>`

issuesFound.title: onClick on non‑interactive element (modal overlay) missing role/button semantics

issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: `<div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}>`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `on()` function (lines 10503–10999) attaches click event listeners to non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` styled as buttons) without providing matching keyboard support. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate these elements using Enter or Space, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The `createElement()` function (lines 10682–10799) creates non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` used as buttons) without assigning an appropriate ARIA role (e.g., `role="button"`) or `tabindex="0"`. These elements are not keyboard-focusable or screen-reader-identifiable, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criteria 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), 2.1.1 (Keyboard), and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected without ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `replaceWith()` function (lines 10746–10799) injects dynamic content into the DOM using `insertAdjacentHTML()` or `replaceChild()` without updating an adjacent ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the change, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.pricing-card a:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel buttons missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons in TestimonialsCarousel (pause, prev, next, dots) lack visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.testimonials-section__pause-btn:focus`, `.testimonials-track__prev:focus`, `.testimonials-track__next:focus`, and `.testimonials-dots__dot:focus` have visible outlines or backgrounds.

issuesFound.title: FaqAccordion trigger buttons missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger buttons in FaqAccordion lack visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.faq-item__trigger:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm submit button lacks visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.newsletter-form button:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The ContactForm submit button lacks visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.contact-form button:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: Footer links missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The Footer links lack visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.site-footer a:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: Back-to-top button missing visible focus styles
issuesFound.explanation: The back-to-top button lacks visible focus styles, which can make it difficult for keyboard users to see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add CSS to ensure `.back-to-top:focus` has a visible outline or background.

issuesFound.title: Base font size too small
issuesFound.explanation: The html element font-size is set to 12px. Since body text uses 1rem, this results in a computed font size of 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability. This affects users with low vision or cognitive disabilities who rely on default text sizing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 137: html { font-size: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a replacement visible style (such as outline-color, outline-style, or box-shadow). This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. Keyboard users will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Users navigating by keyboard cannot identify the focused link within the card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 793: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Search form label hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search label.sr-only selector uses display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users will not hear a label for the search input, violating WCAG 1.3.1.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.5.8 requires a minimum target size of 24x24 pixels for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 623: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Redundant ARIA role on header element
issuesFound.explanation: The header element has role="banner". Native header elements are implicitly mapped to the banner landmark when not nested inside article or section. This role is redundant and adds unnecessary code.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: header { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: var(--z-sticky); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-border); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Redundant ARIA role on footer element
issuesFound.explanation: The footer element has role="contentinfo". Native footer elements are implicitly mapped to the contentinfo landmark. This role is redundant and adds unnecessary code.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1353: footer { background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); padding-top: var(--space-16); }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable the animation, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 733: .hero-image img { transition: none; }
  Line 743: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Testimonial card transition lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card element has box-shadow and border-color transitions. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable these animations for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1233: .testimonial-card { transition: none; }
  Line 1233: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .testimonial-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: .sr-only class exposes content visually

This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only text and clutters the visual interface.

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px degrades text readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size to 12px, which sets the root em (rem) unit to 12px. This results in body text rendering at 12px by default, which is below the 16px minimum recommended for comfortable reading and fails to respect user agent defaults. Users with low vision or those who have difficulty reading small text will struggle to read content without zooming.
issuesFound.fix: Line 117: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative text spacing reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies negative word-spacing (-0.2em) and letter-spacing (-0.05em), compressing text horizontally. This reduces legibility and readability, particularly for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities who rely on clear character and word separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 125: (remove the word-spacing and letter-spacing declarations)

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height too tight for comfortable reading
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height to 1, which provides insufficient vertical spacing between lines of text. This tight spacing makes it difficult for users to track text, especially those with dyslexia or low vision, and fails to meet the WCAG 1.4.8 AAA guideline of at least 1.5 line spacing for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 142: line-height: var(--line-height-normal);

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline to none without providing a replacement visible focus indicator such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the default focus ring from all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to determine which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced colors mode without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Within the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline to none without providing a system-color alternative (such as ButtonText or Highlight). Users relying on Windows High Contrast Mode or other forced color schemes will lose all focus indication, rendering the interface unusable via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 391-393: outline: 2px solid ButtonText;

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced motion media query fails to disable animations
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and contains no rules to disable or reduce the CSS transitions defined throughout the stylesheet (such as those on buttons, cards, and interactive elements). Users who have enabled reduced motion preferences will still experience animations, which can cause vestibular disorders or distraction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: * { transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-delay: 0s !important; animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-delay: 0s !important; }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transitions not disabled for reduced motion preference
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector defines transitions for transform and box-shadow (line 898), but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the perspective rotation and shadow animations on hover, which can cause vestibular issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 903: transition: none;

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class fails to visually hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content in the document flow and fully visible. This defeats the purpose of the class, which is intended to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. Content intended only for screen readers will be displayed to all users, breaking the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414-424: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Search form label removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search label.sr-only applies display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Even if the sr-only class were corrected, this rule would override it and hide the label from assistive technologies. Form inputs without accessible names violate WCAG 4.1.2 and prevent screen reader users from understanding the purpose of the search field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 816: (remove the entire rule block)

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes focus indicators from all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users and violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode (forced colors), this removes the focus indicator entirely, blocking keyboard navigation when users need it most.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class visually exposes content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes content visible to all users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This breaks screen-reader-only text functionality.

issuesFound.title: Focusable screen-reader-only class incorrectly defined
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class uses position: static and width: auto, making focusable elements visible at all times rather than only when focused. This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only text that should become visible on focus.

issuesFound.title: Search form label hidden from all users
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search label.sr-only selector applies display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Combined with the broken .sr-only class, this ensures the search input has no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: (remove this rule entirely)

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically disappear after a fixed duration (up to 6000ms for errors). Users who need more time to read the message cannot pause this timer, violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide). While a dismiss button is provided, it does not pause the auto-dismiss timer when the user hovers over or focuses the toast.
issuesFound.fix: Add mouseenter and mouseleave (or focusin/focusout) listeners to the toast element to clear and restart the dismissal timer, allowing users to pause the notification.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()` or activates it in the `open()` method. When the menu opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the overlay and interact with background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(), initialize the trap: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });`. In open(), activate it: `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`. In close(), ensure deactivation happens: `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.title: Document language attribute not programmatically set
issuesFound.explanation: The application configuration includes `CONFIG.app.locale` (e.g., 'en-GB'), but the code never applies this to the HTML element's `lang` attribute. Screen readers cannot correctly pronounce content without knowing the page language, violating WCAG 3.1.1 (Language of Page).
issuesFound.fix: Add the following line in app.init(): `document.documentElement.lang = CONFIG.app.locale || 'en';`

issuesFound.title: Contact form full name input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form's full name input field is validated but lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling the field with user data, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: In contactForm.init(), after querying the field, add: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` (or ensure the HTML element has `autocomplete="name"`).

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form's email input lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. This prevents automatic filling of the user's email address, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: In contactForm.init(), after querying the field, add: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input is queried but not assigned an `autocomplete` attribute. This prevents browsers from suggesting the user's email, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: In newsletterForm.init(), after `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);`, add: `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard form name input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard's name input field lacks an `autocomplete` attribute, preventing automatic filling of the user's name, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: In multiStepWizard.init(), when binding fields in STEP_FIELDS[0], add: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard form email input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard's email input field lacks an `autocomplete` attribute, preventing automatic filling, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: In multiStepWizard.init(), when binding fields in STEP_FIELDS[0], add: `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle is not a native `<button>`, the code assigns `role="switch"` and `tabindex="0"` but fails to provide an accessible name (via `aria-label` or text content). Screen readers cannot identify the purpose of this control, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: After setting the role, add: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.switchBillingPeriod || 'Switch billing period');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not programmatically communicate its expanded/collapsed state to assistive technologies. The open() and close() functions fail to set aria-expanded on the trigger element, leaving screen reader users unaware whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2680 (in open function): Add after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2700 (in close function): Add after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined within it. Users can tab out of the drawer into the background page content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. The module does not instantiate or activate the createFocusTrap utility available in the codebase.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2720 (in init function): Add after `_initialised = true;`
  `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line 2680 (in open function): Add after `_lockScroll();`
  `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`
  Line 2700 (in close function): Add before `_unlockScroll();`
  `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing initial focus management
issuesFound.explanation: Upon opening the mobile navigation drawer, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Screen reader users may not perceive that the menu has opened or where the new content is located.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2680 (in open function): Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
  `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer;`
  `if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog but lacks aria-modal="true", causing screen readers to not treat it as a modal and potentially not restrict virtual cursor navigation to the drawer boundaries.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2670 (after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`): Add
  `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications disappear automatically after a timeout (default 4000ms for info, 6000ms for errors). Users with cognitive disabilities or who need more time to read cannot pause, stop, or extend this timer, violating WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2418 (in show function, replace the setTimeout line): Replace
  `timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur);`
  with
  `let remaining = dur;`
  `let startTime = Date.now();`
  `timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), remaining);`
  `toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { if (timerId) { clearTimeout(timerId); remaining -= Date.now() - startTime; } });`
  `toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { startTime = Date.now(); timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), remaining); });`
  `toast.addEventListener('focusin', () => { if (timerId) { clearTimeout(timerId); remaining -= Date.now() - startTime; } });`
  `toast.addEventListener('focusout', () => { startTime = Date.now(); timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), remaining); });`

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the CSS properties do the opposite — position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto make content fully visible. This breaks skip links, hidden headings, and any element relying on this class for proper visual hiding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 291-300: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible removes outline without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule removes the default browser focus indicator with outline: none but does not provide any visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or background change. Keyboard-only users cannot see which element has focus, blocking navigation entirely.

issuesFound.title: forced-colors mode :focus-visible removes outline without system color alternative
issuesFound.explanation: In Windows High Contrast Mode, the :focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a visible alternative using system colors like Highlight or ButtonText. Keyboard users in high contrast mode cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: .sr-only class makes content visible instead of hiding it
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the current CSS makes content fully visible (position: static, overflow: visible, clip: auto). This defeats the purpose of the class and may expose content intended only for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Base font size set to 12px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element has font-size: 12px, which sets the root font size below the recommended 16px (1rem) minimum for body text. This makes all rem-based typography smaller than intended and reduces readability for users with visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height set to 1
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph element has line-height: 1, which is below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for readability. This makes text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision who benefit from increased line spacing.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not suppress any transitions or animations. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see all animations and transitions defined elsewhere in the stylesheet.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible focus indicator (no box-shadow, border, or background change). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible to track (WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance).
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. High Contrast Mode users cannot see focus states, blocking keyboard navigation entirely.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of visually hidden. This breaks skip links, hidden labels, and any content meant to be announced by screen readers but hidden visually, causing layout disruption and confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition without reduced-motion suppression
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element has a transform transition but no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress it. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience animation, which can cause vestibular discomfort (WCAG 2.3.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 385: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button transitions without reduced-motion suppression
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element has multiple transitions (background-color, border-color, color, box-shadow, transform) but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress them. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience animations on hover/focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Search input transitions without reduced-motion suppression
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input element has transitions for border-color, box-shadow, and background-color but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress them. Users who prefer reduced motion will experience animations on focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 571: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { #search-input { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition with ineffective reduced-motion guard
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition, and while a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists, it is empty and does not suppress the transition. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform animation on hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs button transitions without reduced-motion suppression
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button has transitions for background-color, color, and box-shadow but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress them. Users preferring reduced motion will experience animations on interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 881: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .filter-tabs button { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button transitions without reduced-motion suppression
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button has transitions for background-color, color, border-color, and box-shadow but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress them. Users preferring reduced motion will experience animations on interaction.

issuesFound.title: Combobox input missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is never set. Screen readers cannot announce whether the suggestion popup is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: add `aria-expanded: 'false'` to the createElement attributes in suggestionListRenderer.mount(), and update aria-expanded to 'true' in show() and 'false' in hide(). Line 2750: in siteSearch._open() add `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true')` and in _close() add `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping and the documentation claims it "Activates a focus trap inside the drawer when open", but the focus trap is never created or activated. When the mobile drawer opens, keyboard focus can escape to content behind the overlay, violating WCAG 2.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1945: in mobileNav.open(), add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`. Also initialize the trap in init(): `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true });`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set when the drawer opens or closes. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1945: in mobileNav.open(), add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 1965: in mobileNav.close(), add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion.close(), aria-expanded is correctly set to 'false' on the trigger, but in open(), aria-expanded is never set to 'true'. This leaves the trigger with an incorrect state after opening, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3565: in faqAccordion.open(), add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element is given role="switch" but aria-checked is never set. Screen readers cannot announce the on/off state of the switch, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3245: in pricing._syncToggleState(), add `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear));` inside the `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack)` block.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tab buttons indicate active state only through CSS class, not through aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell which filter is currently selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2985: in productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(), add `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach callback.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu triggers have aria-haspopup set but aria-expanded is never updated when the dropdown opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2025: in dropdownMenu._openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 2040: in dropdownMenu._closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-expanded state on open
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the `aria-expanded` attribute is not set to "true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", but the open function omits setting it to "true", leaving screen reader users unaware of the expanded state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2310: function open(item, announceToSR) { if (!isPlainObject(item) || item.open) return; item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); addClass(item.el, CSS.open); animateHeight(item.panel, 'auto', { duration : CONFIG.faq.animDuration || 300, easing : 'easeOutCubic', }); item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); ...

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle is assigned `role="switch"` when not a native button, but `aria-checked` is never set or updated. A role="switch" element requires `aria-checked` to communicate its on/off state to assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2100: function _syncToggleState(period) { if (!_periodBtns) return; _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); }); if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); if (_toggleEl.getAttribute('role') === 'switch') { setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear)); } } }

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element in SkipLinks has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple navigation landmarks exist on a page, each must have a unique accessible name so screen reader users can distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~322: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as the dialog role.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1160: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> and Line ~1163: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>
